title: Baby Gear That Lives Well in a Small Space
intro: A 3-month-old in a Salt Lake City condo means every piece of gear has to earn its square footage — natural materials, considered design, nothing that looks like it belongs in a big-box store.

items.title: Pehr Quilted Play Mat
items.note: GOTS-certified organic cotton with natural dyes — it reads as a considered textile, not a piece of baby equipment, and the machine-washable construction means it holds its look through real use.

items.title: Gathre Padded Mini Mat
items.note: The leather-look surface wipes clean in seconds and folds flat when not in use — a well-made double as a changing mat or outdoor blanket that doesn't announce itself as baby gear.

items.title: Ergobaby Embrace Newborn Carrier
items.note: Soft structured fabric sits close and low-profile — no bulky frame, no dangling hardware, and it machines washes without losing its shape through the newborn months.

warnings: Piccalio play mat dropped — foam construction is functionally similar to the Gathre and less distinctive on design; including both would be padding.
Tula Explore sourceUrl points to a collections page (cottonbabies.com/collections/tula-explore-carriers), not a direct product page — flagged for human review before publishing.

Bathtime section dropped entirely in debug mode: Earth Mama and Tubby Todd are consumables with no durable or design dimension — they don't hold up under the 'looks good and holds up' lens and feel like category filler here.
Debug mode: shortlist kept minimal to validate workflow structure and lens application only.
